description_long: This house features a traditional two-story design with a steeply pitched gable roof made of dark brown wood. The exterior walls are finished in white plaster with exposed wooden beams, giving it a classic half-timbered appearance. The lower portion of the house is clad in gray stone, adding a sense of solidity and durability. The front facade includes a central wooden door flanked by two windows on the ground floor and three symmetrically arranged windows on the upper floor. The sides and rear of the house have fewer windows, maintaining a simple and functional look. The overall style evokes a European or Tudor-inspired residential building, suitable for a quaint urban or village setting.
description: A two-story, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, white plaster walls, and a stone base. The front features a central wooden door and symmetrical windows.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entry door, three upper windows, two lower windows, and the prominent gable roof.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wooden roof and a single window on the upper floor, with minimal detailing.
description_view_2: The rear view displays two upper windows and a plain wall, lacking a visible entryway.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the roof and a single upper window.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with no porch, flanked by windows and topped by a simple overhang.
